title: Does background music really increase revenue? — Daniel Fox
meta_description: The effect sizes are large: roughly 15% more per check from turning the volume down, 40% more bar revenue from slower tempo, $4,600 a day in a supermarket. What the revenue research shows, and the conditions behind it.
schema: <script type="application/ld+json">{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"Article","headline":"Does background music really increase revenue?","description":"What a 140-store study revealed about music and revenue: no universal tempo effect, but a real one moderated by store type and customer relationship.","author":{"@type":"Person","name":"Daniel Christopher Fox","alternateName":"Daniel Fox","jobTitle":"Retail Music Behaviorist","url":"https://danielchristopherfox.com"},"publisher":{"@type":"Person","name":"Daniel Christopher Fox"},"mainEntityOfPage":"https://danielchristopherfox.com/notes/does-background-music-increase-revenue.html","about":["Retail music","Revenue","Consumer behavior","Loyalty"]}</script>
